testcase.name: test_discover_get
test_discover_head
test_notification_jsonld
testcase.failure:
self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notification_jsonld> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4364111552'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notification_jsonld(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "application/ld+json"} with open("tests/notification1.json", "r") as f: attrs = {'json.return_value': json.loads("[" + f.read() + "]")} mock_res.configure_mock(**attrs)
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
> notification = Consumer().notification("http://example.org/inbox/1") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_consumer.py:111: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ldnlib.consumer.Consumer object at 0x104379940> iri = 'http://example.org/inbox/1', kwargs = {} headers = {'accept': 'application/ld+json; q=1.0,text/turtle; q=0.9,application/xml+rdf; q=0.5'} response = <urllib3.response.HTTPResponse object at 0x10438c910>
def notification(self, iri, **kwargs): """ Retrieve a single LDN notification and decode into a Python object. """ headers = kwargs.pop("headers", dict()) if 'accept' not in headers: headers['accept'] = kwargs.pop("accept", self.accept_headers)
response = self.http.request('GET', iri, headers=headers, **kwargs) > response.raise_for_status() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'HTTPResponse' object has no attribute 'raise_for_status'
ldnlib/consumer.py:36: AttributeError

testcase.failure:

self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notifications_jsonld_compacted> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4365722560'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notifications_jsonld_compacted(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "application/ld+json"} with open("tests/inbox.jsonld", "r") as f: mock_res.text = f.read()
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
> notifications = Consumer().notifications("http://example.org/inbox") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_consumer.py:37: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ldnlib.consumer.Consumer object at 0x1043ec550> inbox = 'http://example.org/inbox', kwargs = {} headers = {'accept': 'application/ld+json; q=1.0,text/turtle; q=0.9,application/xml+rdf; q=0.5'} response = <urllib3.response.HTTPResponse object at 0x10438d270>
def notifications(self, inbox, **kwargs): """ Retrieve all of the notification IRIs from an ldp:inbox as a list. """ headers = kwargs.pop("headers", dict()) if 'accept' not in headers: headers['accept'] = kwargs.pop("accept", self.accept_headers)
response = self.http.request('GET', inbox, headers=headers, **kwargs) > response.raise_for_status() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'HTTPResponse' object has no attribute 'raise_for_status'
ldnlib/consumer.py:22: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_send_dict
testcase.failure:
self = <test_sender.TestSender testMethod=test_send_dict> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4365725248'>
@patch('requests.post') def test_send_dict(self, mock_post): data = None with open("tests/notification.json", "r") as f: data = json.loads(f.read())
> Sender().send(self.INBOX, data)
tests/test_sender.py:31: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/sender.py:43: in send self.__post_message(inbox, json.dumps(data), self.JSON_LD, _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ldnlib.sender.Sender object at 0x10437af90> inbox = 'http://example.org/inbox' data = '{"@context": {"schema": "http://schema.org/", "creator": {"@id": "schema:creator", "@type": "@id"}}, "creator": "http://example.org/user"}' content_type = 'application/ld+json', kwargs = {} headers = {'content-type': 'application/ld+json'} r = <urllib3.response.HTTPResponse object at 0x10438ec80>
def __post_message(self, inbox, data, content_type, **kwargs): if self.allow_localhost or not self.__is_localhost(inbox): headers = kwargs.pop("headers", dict()) headers['content-type'] = content_type r = self.http.request('POST', inbox, body=data, headers=headers, **kwargs) if r.status < 200 or r.status >= 300: > raise ValueError(f"Request failed with status: {r.status}") E ValueError: Request failed with status: 405
ldnlib/sender.py:36: ValueError

testcase.name: test_send_graph
test_send_list
testcase.failure:
self = <test_sender.TestSender testMethod=test_send_list> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4369025408'>
@patch('requests.post') def test_send_list(self, mock_post): data = None with open("tests/notification.json", "r") as f: data = json.loads("[" + f.read() + "]")
> Sender().send(self.INBOX, data)
tests/test_sender.py:42: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/sender.py:43: in send self.__post_message(inbox, json.dumps(data), self.JSON_LD, _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ldnlib.sender.Sender object at 0x1043ee210> inbox = 'http://example.org/inbox' data = '[{"@context": {"schema": "http://schema.org/", "creator": {"@id": "schema:creator", "@type": "@id"}}, "creator": "http://example.org/user"}]' content_type = 'application/ld+json', kwargs = {} headers = {'content-type': 'application/ld+json'} r = <urllib3.response.HTTPResponse object at 0x1046e8040>
def __post_message(self, inbox, data, content_type, **kwargs): if self.allow_localhost or not self.__is_localhost(inbox): headers = kwargs.pop("headers", dict()) headers['content-type'] = content_type r = self.http.request('POST', inbox, body=data, headers=headers, **kwargs) if r.status < 200 or r.status >= 300: > raise ValueError(f"Request failed with status: {r.status}") E ValueError: Request failed with status: 405
ldnlib/sender.py:36: ValueError

testcase.name: test_send_string
